title: About Me
text:
I’m a research associate / sports scientist based in Eugene in the Bowerman Sport Science Center. I work in a both a clinical and applied research capacity with athletes. In my free time I try to build tools that make data useful, and communicate findings clearly.
My work spans exploratory analysis, statistical modelling, and interactive Shiny applications.


Originally from Spokane, WA I grew up an avid runner before attending Washington State University as a student athlete in cross country & track. I found my love for human performance through this experience. Wanting to understand performance further I attended Marquette University where I worked as a research assistant/analyst before starting at the Bowerman Sport Science Center.




B.A. Social Sciences

Emphasis in Kinesiology, Human Development & Psychology

M.S. Sport & Exercise Analytics

Emphasis in data science








Area
Tools




Data wrangling
dplyr, tidyr, data.table, janitor


Visualization
ggplot2, plotly, gt, reactable


Modelling
lm, glm, lme4, tidymodels


Apps
Shiny, shinydashboard, bslib


Reporting
Quarto, R Markdown, knitr

Git, GitHub







📍 Based in Eugene / Oregon
🔭 Working on translating data into actionable insight
📫 Reach me at zakgkindl@gmail.com

title: My Analysis Title
text: State the question or problem this analysis addresses in 1–2 sentences.

title: My Analysis Title
text: State the question or problem this analysis addresses in 1–2 sentences.

title: My Analysis Title
text:
Data
Describe the dataset and load it here.


Code
library(tidyverse)
library(here)

# Load your data
# data <- read_csv(here("data", "your-data.csv"))

# For demo, use a built-in dataset
data <- palmerpenguins::penguins |> drop_na()

glimpse(data)


Rows: 333
Columns: 8
$ species           <fct> Adelie, Adelie, Adelie, Adelie, Adelie, Adelie, Adel…
$ island            <fct> Torgersen, Torgersen, Torgersen, Torgersen, Torgerse…
$ bill_length_mm    <dbl> 39.1, 39.5, 40.3, 36.7, 39.3, 38.9, 39.2, 41.1, 38.6…
$ bill_depth_mm     <dbl> 18.7, 17.4, 18.0, 19.3, 20.6, 17.8, 19.6, 17.6, 21.2…
$ flipper_length_mm <int> 181, 186, 195, 193, 190, 181, 195, 182, 191, 198, 18…
$ body_mass_g       <int> 3750, 3800, 3250, 3450, 3650, 3625, 4675, 3200, 3800…
$ sex               <fct> male, female, female, female, male, female, male, fe…
$ year              <int> 2007, 2007, 2007, 2007, 2007, 2007, 2007, 2007, 2007…

title: My Analysis Title
text:
Exploration


Code
data |>
  ggplot(aes(x = bill_length_mm, fill = species)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, alpha = 0.7, position = "identity") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#1B6CA8", "#E26A2C", "#2CA85A")) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Bill Length by Species",
    x = "Bill length (mm)",
    y = "Count",
    fill = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "top")





Distribution of key variable

title: My Analysis Title
text:
Analysis
Describe your approach, then show the code.


Code
model <- lm(body_mass_g ~ bill_length_mm + flipper_length_mm + species,
            data = data)

broom::tidy(model, conf.int = TRUE) |>
  filter(term != "(Intercept)") |>
  knitr::kable(digits = 2, caption = "Model coefficients")



Model coefficients
